{{Quote|He's on a collision course. Princess, we're in mortal danger from our own forces.|General Ristt|<ref name="Faithful Wookiee" />}}
[[General/Legends|General]] '''Ristt''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] who served the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] as the ranking [[Officer/Legends|officer]] at an [[Unidentified asteroid field (Rebel Alliance base)|asteroid field]] [[Ring Base|base]] at some point between [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] and [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. When the Rebel-allied [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] returned late to the base from a [[Mission to Panna Prime|mission]] to retrieve the mystical [[Talisman (artifact)|Talisman]] artifact, Ristt aided a [[Unidentified Rebel communications operator 1|communications officer]] in establishing video contact with their [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|freighter]], and subsequently watched in horror as the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] narrowly avoided colliding with the base before speeding away. Fellow rebel [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] went on to retrieve Solo and Chewbacca from the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Panna Prime]], and Ristt later appeared in a [[Unidentified Rebel cartoon|cartoon]] created about the day's events.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Stand by. The ''Falcon'' is near impact.|Ristt, preparing for a collision|<ref name="Faithful Wookiee" />}}
[[File:Eggman.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Luke Skywalker, Ristt, and Leia Organa attempt to communicate with the ''Millennium Falcon''.]]
Ristt<ref name="Bad Feeling">{{InsiderCite|106|I Have a Bad Feeling About This!}}</ref> was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] who served as a [[General/Legends|general]] in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Faithful Wookiee" /> At some point between [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] and [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]<ref name="NEC Date">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' places the [[Mission to Panna Prime|mission]] to [[Panna Prime]] after the [[Evacuation of Yavin]], which it also placed six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], corresponding to [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. {{SW|url=news/the-best-holidays-and-celebrations-in-the-galaxy-everybodys-invited-of-course|text=The Best Holidays and Celebrations in the Galaxy…and Empire Day, Too|int=The Best Holidays and Celebrations in the Galaxy…and Empire Day, Too|nolive=1}} mentions that ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'' was one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after the Battle of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin]], corresponding to [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. Therefore, the events of the mission as portrayed in ''[[The Story of the Faithful Wookiee]]'' must have occurred between 0.5 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]] and 1 ABY.</ref> they were the ranking [[Officer/Legends|officer]] at an Alliance [[Ring Base|base]] located in an [[Unidentified asteroid field (Rebel Alliance base)|asteroid field]]. Under the General's supervision, the Rebel-allied [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] left the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] base on a [[Mission to Panna Prime|mission]] to acquire the [[Talisman (artifact)|Talisman]], a mystical artifact rumored to be sought by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Faithful Wookiee" />

When the smugglers took longer than expected to return, Ristt waited near the base's communications station for a transmission, standing alongside fellow rebels [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], as well as their [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. One [[Unidentified Rebel communications operator 1|officer]] eventually reported the receipt of a weak signal, which materialized into video when Ristt suggested accessing it on a lower channel. The rebels stood puzzled as they watched Solo hang upside-down in the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] of his [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|freighter]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]; they attempted to establish [[Comlink/Legends|radio]] contact with Chewbacca, but their efforts were to no avail.<ref name="Faithful Wookiee" />

Ristt realized that the ''Millennium Falcon'' was on a collision course with the base, but on Organa's insistence that they trust Chewbacca, the [[Communications officer/Legends|communications officer]] held off firing while the group braced themselves for impact. The ''Millennium Falcon'' ultimately passed by the base and continued on its way, which prompted Skywalker and the droids to pursue it in a [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing fighter]]. After tracking the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] to the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Panna Prime]], they discovered that Solo had contracted a [[sleeping virus]] from the Talisman and had been hung upside-down by Chewbacca in order to keep [[Blood/Legends|blood]] flowing to his head. The earlier near-collision had been an attempt by Chewbacca to warn them of the Talisman's drowsy effects.<ref name="Faithful Wookiee" /> A [[Unidentified Rebel cartoon|cartoon]] that chronicled the rebels' adventure on Panna Prime and included an appearance by Ristt was later created, and it was watched by Chewbacca's son [[Lumpawaroo/Legends|Lumpawarrump]] in 1 ABY.<ref name="Holiday Special">''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|This is madness.|Ristt, reacting to the ''Millennium Falcon''{{'s}} impending crash|<ref name="Faithful Wookiee" />}}
Risst was a somewhat portly fellow with a double chin. Although the crest and backside of his cranium were bald, bushy red [[Hair/Legends|sideburns]] connected to a thick mustache of the same [[Color/Legends|color]]. The General additionally sported large red eyebrows; his skin was light; and his [[Eye/Legends|pupils]] were black. Ristt was the first to realize that the ''Millennium Falcon'' was heading straight toward the rebel base, and although they described the situation as "madness," they deferred to Leia Organa's insistence that they trust Chewbacca and not fire on the freighter.<ref name="Faithful Wookiee" />

==Equipment==
While in command of the Alliance base, Ristt wore a white [[Jacket|coat]] over a tan collared [[clothing|shirt]], brown pants, and a brown [[belt]] with a red-colored buckle.<ref name="Faithful Wookiee" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The script revealed that the cartoon's Rebel leader was named General Ristt, apparently a stylized version of a minor character from ''Star Wars''{{'}} Yavin 4 base scenes, judging by the facial hair.|Alex Newborn|<ref name="Bad Feeling" />}}
[[File:Bobh.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Bob Hudsol, sporting some familiar facial hair]]
Ristt's first appearance in ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] came in ''[[The Story of the Faithful Wookiee]]'',<ref name="Faithful Wookiee" /> a cartoon that aired on television in [[1978]] as part of ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]''<ref name="Holiday Special" /> and was later included as an [[Easter egg (virtual)|Easter egg]] on the [[2011]] ''[[Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'' [[Wikipedia:Blu-ray Disc|Blu-ray]] release.<ref name="blu-ray">''[[Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]''</ref> The character was played by an uncredited voice actor.<ref name="Holiday Special" /> Referred to only as "General" in the cartoon,<ref name="Faithful Wookiee" /> Ristt is named in the ''Holiday Special''{{'s}} script.<ref name="script">{{WebCite|url=http://starwarsholidayspecial.com/text/script.htm|text=The Fourth Draft Script - 09/13/78|work=[[Starwarsholidayspecial.com|The Star Wars Holiday Special]]|archivedate=20101223071504}}</ref> The name was later confirmed in ''[[I Have a Bad Feeling About This!]]'', a [[2008]] article published in ''[[Star Wars Insider 106]]''.<ref name="Bad Feeling" />

In the script's fourth draft, Ristt speaks several lines<ref name="script" /> that failed to make the final cut of the cartoon.<ref name="Faithful Wookiee" /> While waiting for Solo to return with the Talisman, they expresses a desire to relocate the asteroid base before being discovered by an Imperial [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], and voices a mistrust of Solo for being a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]]. Upon establishing video contact with the ''Millennium Falcon'', Ristt asserts that Chewbacca has killed Solo and further means to kill the denizens of the rebel base. They explicitly orders the communications officer to fire upon the freighter before being dissuaded by Organa. Later, while Skywalker is searching for the ''Millennium Falcon'', Organa learns that he is in the [[Panna system/Legends|Panna system]] but is told by Ristt that the [[Star system/Legends|system]] contains too many [[Planet/Legends|planets]] and moons, therefore negating the possibility of launching additional rescue [[Starship/Legends|craft]].<ref name="script" /> The line regarding Chewbacca's perceived murderous intent was originally included in the cartoon but was re-dubbed prior to broadcast.<ref name="Bad Feeling" />

[[Alex Newborn]], author of ''I Have a Bad Feeling About This!'', has speculated that Ristt's appearance is a stylized take on a rebel character with similar facial hair from the [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] scenes in the [[1977]] film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']].<ref name="Bad Feeling" /> With a bald head and a thick white sideburns–mustache combo, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Bob Hudsol/Legends|Bob Hudsol]] fits the description;<ref name="Hudsol">{{CCG|set=A New Hope Limited|cardname=Rebel Commander}}</ref> the two characters have additionally been conflated on the fan site [[Starwarsholidayspecial.com]]<ref name="Fan site">{{WebCite|url=http://starwarsholidayspecial.com/characters/ristt.htm|text=General Ristt|work=[[Starwarsholidayspecial.com|The Star Wars Holiday Special]]|archivedate=20101223062637}}</ref> and by Dr. [[Curtis Saxton]] in his [[Curtis Saxton|Star Wars Technical Commentaries]].<ref name="Saxton">{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/swtc/domino.html|text=Rebel Alliance Insignia|author=[[Curtis Saxton]]|date=1997-11-16|work=[[Curtis Saxton|Star Wars Technical Commentaries]]|archivedate=20110720231506}}</ref>
